data.title: Dynamix announces separate trading of its class A ordinary shares and warrants
data.description:
• Dynamix Corporation (NASDAQ:DYNXU) said on Friday that commencing December 9, 2024, holders of the units sold in the company’s initial public offering may elect to separately trade the Class A ordinary shares and warrants included in the units.
  • Class A ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol “DYNX” and “DYNXW,” respectively.
  • Those units not separated will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “DYNXU.”
  • No fractional warrants will be issued upon separation of the units and only whole warrants will trade.

data.title: Roblox continues its green streak for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) continued gains for seven straight sessions as the stock closed 0.61% higher, at $59.30 on Friday.

The online gaming platform company gained more than 19% in the last six trading sessions. The stock has gained more than 28% so far this year, in-line with 27% growth in the broader S&P 500 Index.

RBLX is up 11% over the past one month. The stock closed 8.07% up, at $58.94 on Thursday.

Analysts at Morgan Stanley upgraded Roblox (RBLX) last month to "overweight" following its better than expected earnings and said the company's success is reaching a broader audience and scaling on new platforms, which suggests growing opportunity.

They noted that trends at the company have been steadily improving since Q1, as bookings growth accelerated ~1,500 bps, and that its non-console business contributed 92% of bookings and grew 28% in Q3, nearly 10x faster than the game market in 2024 and the highest level since peak COVID in 2021.

SA Authors and sell-side analysts are bullish on the stock, rating it as a BUY. However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating has a cautious stance, rating the stock a HOLD, with a score of 3.37 out of 5.

Seeking Alpha analyst Selendis Research believes that despite significant user growth and revenue expansion, Roblox remains unprofitable and faces high costs, particularly from platform and developer fees, hindering positive operating income.

“Valuation analysis suggests Roblox is overvalued, with a potential 40% downside, making it prudent for investors to consider taking profits or avoiding new investments,” the analyst wrote.

data.title: Citigroup snaps six consecutive sessions of gain
data.description:
Banking giant Citigroup (NYSE:C) snapped its six-day win streak on Friday, with the stock closing the day 0.15% lower at $72.13.

C had logged six consecutive sessions of gain after the financial services firm, CFRA, said it expects banks to outperform the broader S&P 500 index next year. CFRA had highlighted Citi as a stock on its radar.

The organization stated its belief that the Trump administration will introduce new policies and reduce regulations, which could positively influence capital markets and the investment banking sector.

The stock gained 3.5% over the course of the last six trading days, with a significant rise recorded yesterday. C closed Thursday, December 5, 1.02% higher at $72.23.

"Citigroup is one of the largest banks in the U.S., and the banking sector is one of the most regulated sectors. At any point, regulations can change, which impacts Citigroup negatively, causes their capital requirements to increase, and adds compliance costs," noted Seeking Alpha author Steven Fiorillo in a research note.

"I still think that Citigroup is undervalued and should trade for more than $100 per share," said Fiorillo.

The stock has risen ~40% year-to-date, and is trading close to its 52-week high of $72.85. Short interest stood at 1.78% of the total float as of November 15, representing 33.55M shares sold short.

"The bank continues to invest internally and simplify the business, while retaining (and even growing!) market share in key segments. Once C gets to 'the other side', we expect rapid EPS expansion and continued share price appreciation towards 2030 and beyond," said SA contributor PropNotes.

"While there are some executions, regulatory, and macro risks to be aware of, we think C is an attractive investment opportunity at this point in time. Thus, we're reiterating our 'Buy' rating," the author noted.

SA authors and the sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy.

The New York, NY-based bank found a place in the Wells Fargo "value equity list" of companies that are believed to trade at a discount to their underlying fundamentals and could have a potential to reduce or eliminate that discount.

Meanwhile, SA's Quant Rating system rates C as Hold.

"Rising credit costs, underwhelming RoTCE, and underperforming in some segments like US Personal Banking expose vulnerabilities that cannot be ignored. I think Citi's stock represents a calculated risk. However, it is not for those looking for quick, safe, and stable growth," said SA contributor Shariq Khan.

data.title: Amazon saw engagement with interactive ads climb during the Black Friday NFL game - report
data.description:
According to a Deadline report from Friday, Amazon saw a 10% rise in customer engagement with interactive ads this year during the Black Friday NFL game vs. last year’s debut edition.

The level of engagement on ads was monitored by checking the use of remotes to click and scan interactive messages, the report said. Viewership of the Kansas City Chiefs-Las Vegas Raiders game surged 41% from last year.

There was a 30% year-over-year increase in interactive ads, and about 40% of the brands present in the game were there for the first time, Deadline said, citing the tech giant's internal data points, and about 20% of all advertising featured new creative designed to debut on the game.

“The Black Friday game is changing consumer behaviors for both fans and advertisers as a new sports holiday on one of the biggest shopping days. Prime Video’s leaned-in audience came ready to engage with our Black Friday advertisers through clicks, scans, and search which resulted in our highest customer engagement on record,” Danielle Carney, who oversees Live Sports and Video Sales for Amazon Ads, told Deadline.

Amazon said interactivity in ad messages was 11x more effective in driving engagement vs. just displaying a QR code.

The report claimed Amazon has found viewers on Black Friday were 51% more likely to search for brands or products advertised on the game than viewers of the three NFL Thanksgiving Day games due to the interactive push and the post-holiday timing.

data.title: BofA turns bearish on Liberty Global; cuts rating to 'underperform'
data.description:
• Bank of America downgraded their investment rating on Liberty Global (NASDAQ:LBTYA) to "underperform" from "neutral," saying that the company's remaining strategic initiatives post the Sunrise spinoff "are less convincing" and come with more execution risk.
  • The research firm noted Liberty faces the ongoing challenge of a cable-to-fiber migration and thinks regions could adopt contrasting strategies: building in the UK but moving to full wholesale in Holland.
  • "Notwithstanding Liberty’s strategic challenges, its core assets continue to report mixed operational performance, with the UK and Belgium both expected to report negative FY EBITDA," BofA said on Friday.
  • BofA has a PT of $12.60 for LBTYA, implying a downside of 6%.

data.title: AMD slips as Amazon exec says company 'not yet' seeing heavy demand for AI chips
data.description:
• AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) shares slipped 1.5% late on Friday as an Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) executive told Business Insider that the e-commerce and cloud services giant has not seen enough demand for AMD's artificial intelligence accelerators to sell them via the Amazon Web Services cloud service.
  • "We follow customer demand," Gadi Hutt, who works at Amazon's semiconductor unit Annapurna Labs, told the news outlet. "If customers have strong indications that those are needed, then there's no reason not to deploy."
  • Hutt, who is Annapurna's Director of Product and Customer engineering, added that Amazon Web Services has "not yet" seen high demand for AMD's chips.
  • AMD did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • AMD showed off its first AI accelerator last December, the MI300x. The newest version, the MI325X, is on track to be sent to customers in the fourth-quarter.
  • The company upped its 2024 guidance in October and said it would generate $5B in revenue from its AI accelerators, up from a previous view of $4.5B.

data.title: Lululemon's results and rally draws mixed reviews from Wall Street analysts
data.description:
Investors breathed a sigh and poured into shares of Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) as the company’s Q3 results showed enough improvement in the company’s performance to launch shares as much as 19% higher and into the green for the 11th time in 12 days.

The company, synonymous with yoga pants and athleisure, delivered a solid quarterly earnings report with a 9% increase in sales that bolstered profits by 46% and its gross profit margin 150 basis points. While there were a few soft spots – U.S. comparable sales were down 2% -- the company enjoyed significant gains overseas.

The outcome of Q3 translated into an upbeat outlook for the remainder of the year, as well, with revenue growth pegged at 8% to 10% for Q4.

The consensus from the sell side, however, was mixed as Citi analyst Paul Lejuez warned that after years of benefitting from outsized growth in active apparel, “trends in the category have slowed with data in yoga and active apparel pointing to further deceleration in Q2 2025,” leaving Lululemon (LULU) more susceptible to promotional pricing pressures from competitors. Lejuez is also concerned with the long-term outlook for China, as “incremental weakening of the China consumer environment” is an added risk for Lululemon (LULU).

Wells Fargo’s Ike Boruchow is more cautiously optimistic on Lululemon’s (LULU) prospects and advised bulls to remain patient for an early 2025 inflection. The “no worse-than-feared” print shows that management may have their arms around merchandising and execution issues that plagued the company this year. Boruchow views the Q3 results as “more in line than better,” and views the slowdown in North America along with “over earning” profit & loss a reason to remain cautious over the next 12 months. Boruchow maintained his Equalweight rating on the stock.

No surprise, the results failed to move the needle for perennial Lululemon (LULU) bear, Randal Konik at Jefferies. “With increasing competition and fashion trends shifting away from [Lululemon’s] core offering, we advise investors to be sellers on the uptick in shares today,” he says, citing declining U.S. comparable sales, the choppy macro environment, and the men’s segment slowing to below 10% growth, a development Konik calls a “yellow flag.” Konik held onto his Underperform rating and $220 price target, which is 45% below LULU’s current price.

But for Morgan Stanley’s Alex Straton, the 2% decline in North American sales is reason to double down on her bullish thesis for Lululemon (LULU) and to up her price target by 20% to $414.

The comparable sales in the Americas suggest the region may be bottoming as strategic newness and product decisions appear to be taking hold “leaving us more confident that a re-acceleration in the business is possible in 2025,” and the conservative guidance “sets LULU up for another beat and raise in Q4,” Straton says.

“Taken together, the quarter emboldened our Overweight thesis, and we think likely shifts key debates in favor of the bulls.”

The consensus among Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors appear to agree with Straton, giving Lululemon (LULU) an average Buy rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views Lululemon (LULU) as a Hold.

data.title: Bitcoin set to rise on weekly basis, soars 132% in a year
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to rise 4.32% on a weekly basis, as it finally breached the $100K threshold in the period, after hovering around it for some weeks.

The world’s largest digital currency has seen a meteoric rise since Donald Trump won the elections, and investors had been waiting for the cryptocurrency to jet past the $100K mark.

BTC started the week over $95K, and traded in a narrow range till Wednesday.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) touched the long awaited milestone as Trump’s pick of crypto backer, Paul Atkins as the SEC chair on Wednesday played the catalyst in pushing it past the mark.

The cryptocurrency, however, fell below the mark on Thursday.

“If Donald Trump's plan to create a Bitcoin strategic reserve is implemented and other countries quickly follow suit, it would significantly increase overall demand for Bitcoin. This could make Bitcoin worth $400,000 per coin within the next 5 years,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Kennan Mell.

Click here to know what other Seeking Alpha analysts are saying as Bitcoin traded above $100K.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is up a whopping 132% in a year.

"As long as Bitcoin maintains this close—over $100k—going into the end of this week, we would expect that it continues to run higher, and could easily see it between $110k and $120k this month and going into next year," Cory Klippsten, CEO of Swan, a Bitcoin financial services firm, said.

With the cryptocurrency’s rise, it is becoming one of the most prominent "Trump trades" in the aftermath of the 2024 election. Since the polls, Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) price has jumped ~50%, as it benefits from rise in demand, increase in ETF inflows and a positive investor sentiment fuelled by Trump’s win.

Wednesday also saw robust inflows into the spot BTC ETFs with $556.8 million coming in as net inflows, according to data from Farside Investors.

"As we look ahead, Bitcoin's potential for growth remains significant, especially as institutions recognize it as a reliable store of value and increasingly invest in Bitcoin ETFs. Additionally, we anticipate interest in Ethereum ETFs and the forthcoming approval of Solana and Ripple ETFs, which will further diversify institutional portfolios," said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE.

Companies such as MicroStrategy (MSTR) have also been on a Bitcoin purchasing spree, and more companies have been looking to join the bandwagon, leading to a rise in corporate adoption of the cryptocurrency.

Institutional flows into Bitcoin (BTC-USD) are expected to continue in 2025, which would make the cryptocurrency reach $200,000 by the end of next year, said Geoff Kendrick, head of Crypto Research at Standard Chartered Bank, in a note.

"I think the prospects for next year also look excellent, though this depends a lot more on whether we get the strategic Bitcoin reserve and other political outcomes, with some these outcomes potentially sending Bitcoin into the hundreds of thousands of dollars per coin,” Klippsten added.

Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) market cap now stands at $1.97 trillion.

"Technical analysis would point to about 120K as the next immediate resistance, and we would expect to see more major altcoins start to outperform in the near term as the FOMO appetite spreads out," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.

Notable News

CleanSpark (CLSK) mined 622 bitcoins in November, while Argo Blockchain (ARBK) Wednesday said that it mined 39 Bitcoin in the month. Riot Platforms (RIOT) produced 495 Bitcoin in November. MARA mined 26.7% more Bitcoin in the month.

MARA (MARA) said it will acquire a wind farm in Hansford County, Texas. The firm announced the pricing of its offering of $850 million aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031.

Nano Labs (NA) said it plans to purchase and hold $50 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the next five years.

The U.S. will likely approve cryptocurrency legislation "fairly quickly" after crypto-friendly Donald Trump becomes president, Coinbase's (COIN) policy chief Faryar Shirzad told CNBC.

Coinbase (COIN) added Apple Pay (AAPL) for all fiat-to-crypto purchases to its Onramp product for onboarding to crypto, as it seeks to streamline what can be a cumbersome process.

Bitcoin, Ether prices

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) up 2.58% to ~$101.70K at 3.05 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) rose 6.40% to $4.07K.

The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Imperial Petroleum (IMPP) and Cellectar Biosciences (CLRB). Short interest is elevated on B. data.title: Bloom Energy price target hiked to Street high at Susquehanna, seeing data center momentum
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) +4.1% in Friday's trading as Susquehanna reiterates its Positive rating while raising its price target to a Street-high $33 from $20, to reflect the company's momentum in supplying fuel cells to power data centers and other large industrial customers.

"Onsite generation using Bloom's fuel cell technology is emerging as a real alternative to traditional grid power for consumers who are struggling to secure grid power in a timely manner," Susquehanna's Biju Perincheril writes.

The move comes three weeks after American Electric Power (AEP) said it would use up to 1 GW of Bloom's (BE) fuel cells to power data centers, which the analyst says should pave the way for additional large utility-level agreements.

The order "serves as a proof point for Bloom's fuel cells as a viable option for data centers looking for quick access to power in the face of significant grid constraints on power availability," Perincheril writes, believing "this method of utilities using Bloom fuel cells for distributed generation will be increasingly common."
